code: @Manual{,   title = {eepR: eepR: Common Functions that I Use Regularly},   author = {Ekarin Eric Pongpipat},   year = {2026},   note = {R package version 0.6.0},   url = {https://ekarinpongpipat.com/eepR}, }

what: eepR: Common Functions that I Use Regularly
title: eepR: Common Functions that I Use Regularly
text: package contains helper functions use regularly. Note: package likely always testing phase. Please use caution.

title: eepR: Common Functions that I Use Regularly
apa_table — apa_table
text: black border top bottom columns, black border bottom table, font Times size 12

title: apa_table — apa_table
code: apa_table(renamed_tidy(lm(mpg ~ wt, data = mtcars)))     lh       op       rh       b       se       t       p     mpg ~ (Intercept) 37.285126 1.877627 19.857575 8.241799e-19mpg ~ wt -5.344472 0.559101 -9.559044 1.293959e-10

title: append_colnames — append_colnames
text: data data.frame contains column names change prefix prefix string add beginning column names data.frame suffix string add end column names data.frame

title: append_colnames — append_colnames
text: data.frame new column names prefix string appended column names

title: append_colnames — append_colnames
code: df <- append_colnames(carData::Salaries, prefix = 'p_', suffix = '_s') head(df) #>    p_rank_s p_discipline_s p_yrs.since.phd_s p_yrs.service_s p_sex_s p_salary_s #> 1      Prof              B                19              18    Male     139750 #> 2      Prof              B                20              16    Male     173200 #> 3  AsstProf              B                 4               3    Male      79750 #> 4      Prof              B                45              39    Male     115000 #> 5      Prof              B                40              41    Male     141500 #> 6 AssocProf              B                 6               6    Male      97000

title: calculate_sdt — calculate_sdt
text: calculate signal detection theory (SDT) metrics d' c

title: calculate_sdt — calculate_sdt
text: n_tp number true positives (hit) n_tn number true negatives (correct rejection) n_fp number false positives (false alarm) n_fn number false negatives (miss)

title: calculate_sdt — calculate_sdt
text: data.frame signal detection theory metrics along variables created along way

title: calculate_sdt — calculate_sdt
code: calculate_sdt(25, 26, 5, 4) #> # A tibble: 1 × 14 #>       n   n_t   n_f accuracy   tpr   tnr   fpr   fnr tpr_adj fpr_adj tpr_z #>   <dbl> <dbl> <dbl>    <dbl> <dbl> <dbl> <dbl> <dbl>   <dbl>   <dbl> <dbl> #> 1    60    51     9     0.85 0.862 0.839 0.161 0.138   0.862   0.161  1.09 #> # ℹ 3 more variables: fpr_z <dbl>, d_prime <dbl>, c <dbl>

title: check_contrast_orthogonality — check_contrast_orthogonality
text: checks make sure contrast factor orthogonal.

title: check_contrast_orthogonality — check_contrast_orthogonality
text: checks make sure contrast factor orthogonal ensuring (1) sum contrast equals 0 (2) sum product contrast pair equals 0

title: check_contrast_orthogonality — check_contrast_orthogonality
code: contrast_example <- data.frame(c1 = c(1/3, 1/3, -2/3),                                c2 = c(-1/2, 1/2, 0)) check_contrast_orthogonality(contrast_example) #> rule 1. sum of each contrast equals 0 ✓  #> rule 2. sum of products of contrast pairs equals 0 ✓  #> [1] TRUE

title: check_out_paths — check_out_paths

title: compare_all_models — compare_all_models
code: data <- carData::Salaries model_1 <- lm(salary ~ yrs.since.phd, data) model_2 <- lm(salary ~ yrs.since.phd * rank, data) model_3 <- lm(salary ~ yrs.since.phd * rank * sex, data) compare_all_models(list(model_1, model_2, model_3)) #> # A tibble: 3 × 12 #>   model formula           n   n_p     sse      ssr  df_r df_residual f_statistic #>   <dbl> <chr>         <int> <int>   <dbl>    <dbl> <int>       <int>       <dbl> #> 1     1 salary ~ yrs…   397     2 2.99e11 NA          NA         395      NA     #> 2     2 salary ~ yrs…   397     6 2.19e11  8.05e10     4         391      35.9   #> 3     3 salary ~ yrs…   397    12 2.17e11  1.79e 9     6         385       0.529 #> # ℹ 3 more variables: r_sqaure <dbl>, adj_r_squared <dbl>, p.value <dbl>

title: compare_brains — compare_brains
code: compare_brains(   a,   b,   mask,   ci = 0.95,   legend_title = "Intensity",   out_path = NULL,   out_width = 14,   out_height = 7,   ... )

title: compare_brains — compare_brains
text: first image compare b second image compare mask mask image ci confidence interval bland-altman plots (default: 0.95) legend_title legend title (default: intensity) out_path path save image out_width width saved image inches (default: 11) out_height height saved image inches (default: 8.5) ... additional parameters plot_brain()

title: compare_models — compare_models
text: model_1 model compare model_2 model compare

title: compare_models — compare_models
code: data <- carData::Salaries model_1 <- lm(salary ~ yrs.since.phd * rank, data) model_2 <- lm(salary ~ yrs.since.phd * rank * sex, data) compare_models(model_1, model_2) #> # A tibble: 2 × 12 #>   model formula            n   n_p     sse     ssr  df_r df_residual f_statistic #>   <dbl> <chr>          <int> <int>   <dbl>   <dbl> <int>       <int>       <dbl> #> 1     1 salary ~ yrs.…   397     6 2.19e11 NA         NA         391      NA     #> 2     2 salary ~ yrs.…   397    12 2.17e11  1.79e9     6         385       0.529 #> # ℹ 3 more variables: r_sqaure <dbl>, adj_r_squared <dbl>, p.value <dbl>

title: cor2apa — cor2apa
text: m model cor.test() level specify confidence level (default: 0.95). note: level match confidence level used cor.test(). argument actually update confidence interval range, reported confidence interval level digits round statistics specified digit format specify format (default: html) (options: html, plain)

title: cv_kfold_split_file — cv_kfold_split_file
text: x_path path predictor CSV file y_path path outcome CSV file k_fold number folds out_dir output directory split CSV files

title: cv_kfold_split_file — cv_kfold_split_file
text: Invisibly returns NULL; writes k-fold train/test CSV files out_dir.

title: cv_kfold_split_file — cv_kfold_split_file
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ tmp <- tempdir() x_path <- file.path(tmp, "x.csv") y_path <- file.path(tmp, "y.csv") write.csv(iris[, 1:4], x_path, row.names = FALSE) write.csv(iris["Species"], y_path, row.names = FALSE) cv_kfold_split_file(x_path, y_path, k_fold = 5, out_dir = file.path(tmp, "cv")) } # }

code: initialize_optparse("pmid2doi") #> #!/usr/local/bin/Rscript --vanilla #>  #> suppressMessages(require(optparse)) #> option_list <- list( #>   make_option(opt_str = c('--pmid')) #> ) #> opts <- parse_args(OptionParser(option_list = option_list)) #> pmid2doi(opts$pmid)

title: is_centered — is_centered
text: x numeric vector matrix eps small error tolerance close enough zero (default: 1e-8)

title: is_scaled — is_scaled
text: Checks numeric vector matrix (column-wise) scaled according specified type

title: is_scaled — is_scaled
text: x numeric vector matrix type type scaling check . Options : "mean_centered" ("centered"), "z_scored" ("z"), "ss1". Default "z". eps small error tolerance close enough zero (default: 1e-8)

text: Checks numeric vector matrix (column-wise) sum squares equal 1 (within specified tolerance)

title: is_ss1 — is_ss1

title: lmer2apa — lmer2apa
code: lmer2apa(lmerTest::lmer(Reaction ~ 1 + Days + (1 + Days | Subject), lme4::sleepstudy), format = 'plain') #> Warning: 'oldNames' is deprecated. Please use 'signames' instead. #> Computing profile confidence intervals ... #>                                                                                         (Intercept)  #> "b = 251.405, t(174) = 36.838, p = 1.17e-17, 95% CI [237.681, 265.130], Adjusted R-Squared = 0.987"  #>                                                                                                Days  #>      "b = 10.467, t(174) = 6.771, p = 3.26e-06, 95% CI [7.359, 13.576], Adjusted R-Squared = 0.714"

what: mat2nii Reconstruct a NIfTI File from a 2D Time-by-Voxel Matrix — mat2nii
title: mat2nii Reconstruct a NIfTI File from a 2D Time-by-Voxel Matrix — mat2nii
text: Takes 2D matrix rows represent timepoints (directions) columns represent voxels, projects back 3D/4D NIfTI space. Uses required reference image inherit spatial dimensions orientation headers. optional binary mask provided, voxels mapped back specific coordinates.

title: mat2nii Reconstruct a NIfTI File from a 2D Time-by-Voxel Matrix — mat2nii
code: mat2nii(   matrix_2d,   in_reference,   in_mask = NULL,   out_path = NULL,   overwrite = FALSE )

title: mat2nii Reconstruct a NIfTI File from a 2D Time-by-Voxel Matrix — mat2nii
text: matrix_2d 2D numeric matrix dimensions [T x V]. in_reference Path 3D NIfTI file used master spatial geometry orientation template (e.g., T1w reference functional volume). Required. in_mask Optional path 3D binary mask NIfTI file. provided, matrix columns mapped back active mask coordinates. NULL, columns mapped full volume. out_path Optional input path save reconstructed NIfTI file (.nii .nii.gz). overwrite Logical. TRUE, overwrites existing file out_path. Default FALSE.

title: mat2nii Reconstruct a NIfTI File from a 2D Time-by-Voxel Matrix — mat2nii
text: internalimage object RNifti package, invisibly returned         written disk, directly returned out_path NULL.

title: mat2nii Reconstruct a NIfTI File from a 2D Time-by-Voxel Matrix — mat2nii
code: # Example 1: Reconstructing a masked time-series using a T1 reference and a brain mask # nii_obj <- mat2nii(bold_matrix, in_reference = "T1w.nii.gz", in_mask = "brain_mask.nii.gz")  # Example 2: Reconstructing an unmasked, full-volume matrix using a T1 reference # nii_obj <- mat2nii(full_matrix, in_reference = "T1w.nii.gz", in_mask = NULL)

Please use 'signames' instead. #> Computing profile confidence intervals ... #>                                                                                         (Intercept)  #> "b = 251.405, t(174) = 36.838, p = 1.17e-17, 95% CI [237.681, 265.130], Adjusted R-Squared = 0.987"  #>                                                                                                Days  #>      "b = 10.467, t(174) = 6.771, p = 3.26e-06, 95% CI [7.359, 13.576], Adjusted R-Squared = 0.714"

what: nii2mat Convert a NIfTI File into a 2D Time-by-Voxel Matrix — nii2mat
title: nii2mat Convert a NIfTI File into a 2D Time-by-Voxel Matrix — nii2mat
text: Reads 3D 4D NIfTI volume, applies spatial 3D binary mask available, flattens -mask data 2D matrix. last dimension (Time Diffusion direction) structured rows, active spatial voxels structured columns. Handles single-volume (3D) inputs returning 1-row matrix.

title: nii2mat Convert a NIfTI File into a 2D Time-by-Voxel Matrix — nii2mat
code: nii2mat(in_path, in_mask = NULL, out_path = NULL, overwrite = FALSE)

title: nii2mat Convert a NIfTI File into a 2D Time-by-Voxel Matrix — nii2mat
text: in_path Input path 3D 4D NIfTI file. in_mask Input path 3D binary mask NIfTI file. Optional. NULL, voxels included. out_path Optional input path save 2D matrix output (.rds format recommended). overwrite Logical. TRUE, overwrites existing file out_path. Default FALSE.

title: nii2mat Convert a NIfTI File into a 2D Time-by-Voxel Matrix — nii2mat
nii2mat Convert a NIfTI File into a 2D Time-by-Voxel Matrix — nii2mat

title: plot_heatmap — plot_heatmap
text: Plot affine/adjacency matrix (e.g., correlation matrix) BIDS-format lookup table, grouping bands, grid lines.

title: plot_heatmap — plot_heatmap
code: plot_heatmap(   affine_matrix,   lut,   group_var = "network",   border_width = 10,   scale_fill_type = c("gradient2", "viridis", "distiller"),   scale_fill_limits = NULL,   scale_fill_low = "blue",   scale_fill_mid = "white",   scale_fill_high = "red",   scale_fill_midpoint = 0,   scale_fill_palette = NULL,   na_val = NULL,   scale_fill_na_color = "grey50",   diagonal_to_na = FALSE,   title = NULL,   subtitle = NULL,   x_label = NULL,   y_label = NULL,   x_breaks = NULL,   y_breaks = NULL,   out_path = NULL,   width = 7,   height = 6,   units = "in",   dpi = 300,   match_by_name = TRUE,   diamond = FALSE,   diamond_direction = c("vertical", "horizontal"),   grid_color = "black",   grid_linewidth = 0.5,   legend_title = "Value",   group_legend_title = group_var )

title: plot_heatmap — plot_heatmap
text: affine_matrix square matrix, data.frame, path CSV/TSV file containing matrix. Can without row column names. lut data.frame path BIDS-format TSV file containing lookup table. Must contain 'index' 'color' (hex values) columns, grouping variable column. group_var Character. Name column lut use grouping variable. Default "network". border_width Integer. Width (plot units/pixels) grouping bands around plot. Set 0 omit. Default 10. scale_fill_type Character. Type color scale use: "gradient2" (diverging), "viridis", "distiller". Default "gradient2". scale_fill_limits Numeric vector length 2. Minimum maximum values colorscale. Default NULL (auto-calculated). scale_fill_low Character. Color low values "gradient2" scale. Default "blue". scale_fill_mid Character. Color middle values "gradient2" scale. Default "white". scale_fill_high Character. Color high values "gradient2" scale. Default "red". scale_fill_midpoint Numeric. Midpoint "gradient2" scale. Default 0. scale_fill_palette Character. Name palette/option use "viridis" "distiller" scale. Default NULL. na_val Numeric. Value(s) matrix replace NA. Default NULL. scale_fill_na_color Character. Color use NA values matrix. Default "grey50". diagonal_to_na Logical. TRUE, sets diagonal elements matrix NA. Default FALSE. title Character. Title plot. Default NULL. subtitle Character. Subtitle plot. Default NULL. x_label Character. Label x-axis. Default NULL. y_label Character. Label y-axis. Default NULL. x_breaks Numeric vector. Breaks x-axis. Default NULL (defaults c(1, N)). y_breaks Numeric vector. Breaks y-axis. Default NULL (defaults c(1, N)). out_path Character. Optional path save generated plot. NULL, plot saved. width Numeric. Width output image units. Default 7. height Numeric. Height output image units. Default 6. units Character. Units output image size ("", "cm", "mm", "px"). Default "". dpi Numeric. Resolution output image dots per inch. Default 300. match_by_name Logical. TRUE, attempts align reorder matrix rows/columns match lookup table using colnames/rownames matching. Default TRUE. diamond Logical. TRUE, heatmap rotated 45 degrees display diamond shape. Default FALSE. diamond_direction Character. Direction main diagonal diamond view: "vertical" (default) "horizontal". grid_color Character. Color grid lines. Default "black". grid_linewidth Numeric. Width grid lines. Default 0.5. legend_title Character. Title heatmap color legend. Default "Value". group_legend_title Character. Title grouping band legend. Default matches group_var.

title: plot_heatmap — plot_heatmap
code: # Simulate a 400-node lookup table matching Schaefer 400 with Yeo 7 networks networks <- c("Visual", "Somatomotor", "Dorsal Attention", "Ventral Attention",               "Limbic", "Frontoparietal", "Default") net_colors <- c("#781286", "#4682B4", "#00760E", "#C43A4D",                 "#FFFFC4", "#E69422", "#CD3E4E") net_rep <- rep(networks, each = 57, length.out = 400) color_rep <- rep(net_colors, each = 57, length.out = 400)  sim_lut <- data.frame(   index = 1:400,   name = paste0("parcel", 1:400),   color = color_rep,   network = net_rep,   stringsAsFactors = FALSE )  # Simulate a 400x400 symmetric correlation matrix with network block structures set.seed(42) sim_matrix <- matrix(runif(400 * 400, min = -0.2, max = 0.2), nrow = 400, ncol = 400) sim_matrix <- (sim_matrix + t(sim_matrix)) / 2 for (net in networks) {   idx <- which(sim_lut$network == net)   sim_matrix[idx, idx] <- sim_matrix[idx, idx] + runif(length(idx)^2, min = 0.3, max = 0.7) } diag(sim_matrix) <- 1.0 rownames(sim_matrix) <- sim_lut$name colnames(sim_matrix) <- sim_lut$name  # Plot the heatmap p <- plot_heatmap(   affine_matrix = sim_matrix,   lut = sim_lut,   group_var = "network",   border_width = 10,   diagonal_to_na = TRUE,   title = "Simulated Schaefer 400 (Yeo 7 Networks)" ) print(p)   # Plot the same heatmap rotated to be a diamond shape p_diamond <- plot_heatmap(   affine_matrix = sim_matrix,   lut = sim_lut,   group_var = "network",   border_width = 10,   diagonal_to_na = TRUE,   diamond = TRUE,   title = "Rotated Simulated Schaefer 400" ) print(p_diamond)

title: tidy_colnames — tidy_colnames

title: warning_msg — warning_msg
text: Invisibly returns NULL; prints formatted warning message.
